title: Garlicky Smoky Polish Beans Galette
description: A keto-friendly, comforting Polish dish featuring Long Beans in a creamy, smoky sauce.

ingredients.name: Long beans
ingredients.notes: any color, but green is traditional

ingredients.name: Smoked bacon
ingredients.notes: look for thinly sliced, or chop thicker slices

ingredients.name: Heavy cream
ingredients.notes: or substitute with full-fat coconut milk for a dairy-free option

ingredients.name: Cheddar cheese
ingredients.notes: shredded, or use a vegan alternative

instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Cut the long beans into 1-inch pieces and place them on the prepared baking sheet. Roast in the preheated oven for 15 minutes, or until tender and lightly browned.
In a large skillet, cook the smoked bacon over medium heat until crispy. Remove from heat and set aside.
In the same skillet, cook the sliced onion over medium heat until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the flour to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Gradually pour in the heavy cream, whisking continuously to prevent lumps. Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until thickened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the shredded cheddar cheese until melted and smooth. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
To assemble the galette, arrange the roasted long beans on a large plate or skillet. Spoon the creamy sauce over the top.
Top the galette with crispy bacon and chopped onion.
